title: The Core Pillars of Cryptography for Network Security
description: A flowchart illustrating the fundamental cryptographic processes and components used to secure data in transit over a network, ensuring confidentiality, integrity, and authentication.
relationships: Plaintext is encrypted using an algorithm and a key to produce ciphertext
Ciphertext is transmitted over an insecure network
Recipient decrypts ciphertext using a matching algorithm and key to recover plaintext
Hash functions create a unique fingerprint of data to verify integrity
Digital signatures use asymmetric cryptography to authenticate the sender and ensure non-repudiation
